insights.finding: Restaurant managers spend 3+ hours weekly on manual labor scheduling
evidence: I spend half my Monday just doing the schedule
We use paper and pencil still, it takes forever
Scheduling is the worst part of my week
I have to call everyone individually to check availability
It takes about 3-4 hours each week, every week
Sometimes I stay late just to finish the schedule

insights.finding: Labor costs average 30-35% of revenue, with overtime being the primary budget leak
evidence: Mean labor cost ratio: 32.4%
Overtime accounts for 8.2% of total labor cost
Top quartile restaurants keep labor below 28%
Overtime spikes correlate with understaffing on weekends
Average overtime cost per location: $2,400/month

insights.finding: Shift swaps and last-minute changes cause 60% of scheduling conflicts
evidence: People call out last minute and I have to scramble
Shift swaps happen 3-4 times a week minimum
The biggest problem is when someone doesn't show up
We need a better way to handle swap requests
Last-minute changes throw off the whole week

insights.finding: Managers want real-time visibility into labor costs vs budget
evidence: I never know if I'm over budget until end of pay period
Would love to see a live counter of labor spend
We get surprised by overtime costs every month

insights.finding: Compliance with break laws and max-hour regulations is a top concern
evidence: We got fined once for break violations
Keeping track of California labor laws is a nightmare
I need alerts when someone is approaching overtime

insights.finding: Staff prefer mobile-first schedule access over printed schedules
evidence: Average mobile preference rating: 4.2/5
72% of staff check schedule on their phone
Only 15% prefer the printed schedule on the wall

insights.finding: Peak labor demand mismatches current staffing by 20% during Friday-Saturday dinner
evidence: Friday dinner revenue 40% higher than weekday average
Saturday requires 2.3x weekday staffing levels
Understaffing on weekends causes 15% longer wait times
Current scheduling doesn't account for seasonal demand patterns
Labor gap analysis shows consistent weekend understaffing

insights.finding: Multi-location operators need consolidated view across all restaurants
evidence: I manage 3 locations and can never see the big picture
Would be great to compare labor costs across stores

insights.finding: Employee availability tracking is mostly done via text messages and memory
evidence: People text me their availability randomly
I keep it all in my head which is terrible
We have a shared Google Sheet but nobody updates it

insights.finding: Average employee satisfaction score: 3.1/5 regarding current scheduling process
evidence: Mean satisfaction: 3.1
Min: 1
Max: 5
Standard deviation: 1.2
Only 18% rated satisfaction 4 or above

personas.name: Restaurant GM
personas.role: General manager responsible for day-to-day operations and scheduling
goals: Keep labor costs under 30% of revenue
Ensure proper staffing during peak hours
painPoints: Manual scheduling takes 3+ hours per week
No real-time visibility into labor costs
behaviors: Uses spreadsheets or paper for scheduling
Handles shift swaps via phone calls and texts
Reviews labor costs at end of pay period

personas.name: Multi-Unit Operator
personas.role: Oversees 3-5 restaurant locations with district-level P&L responsibility
behaviors: Reviews weekly P&L for each location
Visits each location 1-2 times per week
Relies on GMs for scheduling decisions

themes.name: scheduling
themes.description: Core scheduling workflow pain points — manual process, shift swaps, and demand mismatch

themes.name: labor-cost
themes.description: Labor cost visibility, overtime tracking, and budget management

themes.name: pain-point
themes.description: User frustrations with current tools and processes

themes.description: Data-backed findings from surveys, POS data, and financial analysis

themes.name: qualitative
themes.description: Interview and observational findings from user research sessions

themes.name: staff-experience
themes.description: Employee-side experience with scheduling and communication
